chapter_context.summary: Contrasts the cursed man who trusts in human flesh with the blessed man who trusts in the Lord, discusses the deceitful heart, and urges keeping the Sabbath.

outline.text: Judah's Sin Written with Pen of Iron and Diamond Point
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judah's Sin Written with Pen of Iron and Diamond Point (17:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Trust of Man vs. Trust of God; Deceitful Heart of Man
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Trust of Man vs. Trust of God; Deceitful Heart of Man (17:5-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Sanctuary Throne of Glory; Pleading for Personal Healing
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sanctuary Throne of Glory; Pleading for Personal Healing (17:12-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Exhortation to Keep and Hallow Sabbath at City Gates
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Exhortation to Keep and Hallow Sabbath at City Gates (17:19-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 17, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
